cards.question: Current : Current is the flow of particles in a particular direction. E.g air currents that cause winds or water currents.
cards.text_html: Current: Current is the flow of particles in a particular direction. E.g air currents that cause winds or water currents.

cards.question: The flow of electric charges in a circuit is called an electric current . The direction is taken from the positive terminal to the negative terminal of the battery in an external circuit.
cards.text_html: The flow of electric charges in a circuit is called an electric current. The direction is taken from the positive terminal to the negative terminal of the battery in an external circuit.

cards.question: A device that produces a small amount of electricity is called an electric cell . It is called a dry cell because it does not contain any liquid chemical.
cards.text_html: A device that produces a small amount of electricity is called an electric cell. It is called a dry cell because it does not contain any liquid chemical.

cards.question: The electric cell is a cylindrical device having a small metal cap on side and a metal disc on the other side. It has two terminals – positive and negative terminal. The metal cap is a positive terminal and is marked (+) whereas the metal disc is a negative terminal and is marked (-).
cards.text_html: The electric cell is a cylindrical device having a small metal cap on side and a metal disc on the other side. It has two terminals – positive and negative terminal. The metal cap is a positive terminal and is marked (+) whereas the metal disc is a negative terminal and is marked (-).

cards.question: The electric cell produces electricity from the chemicals stored inside it. When all the chemicals in the electric cell are used up, the electric cell stops producing electricity.
cards.text_html: The electric cell produces electricity from the chemicals stored inside it. When all the chemicals in the electric cell are used up, the electric cell stops producing electricity.

cards.question: The electric cells or batteries used as a source of electricity in torches, wristwatches, etc. can be recharged again.
cards.text_html: The electric cells or batteries used as a source of electricity in torches, wristwatches, etc. can be recharged again.

cards.question: Battery: When two or more cells are joined together, the combination is called a battery.
cards.text_html: Battery: When two or more cells are joined together, the combination is called a battery.

cards.question: Dry cell: Dry cell is a source of electricity. It generates a direct current (DC) due to a chemical reaction that takes place inside it.
cards.text_html: Dry cell: Dry cell is a source of electricity. It generates a direct current (DC) due to a chemical reaction that takes place inside it.

topics.topic_name: A Bulb Connected to an Electric cell

cards.question: The torch bulb consists of a small glass bulb fixed on a metal case. A thin wire called filament is fixed between two fixed wires inside the glass bulb. A filament is made up of very thin tungsten wire.
cards.text_html: The torch bulb consists of a small glass bulb fixed on a metal case. A thin wire called filament is fixed between two fixed wires inside the glass bulb. A filament is made up of very thin tungsten wire.

cards.question: The filament of the bulb glows when electricity from a cell is passed through it. The two thick wires inside the glass bulb provide support to the filament and make the connection of the filament to the outer circuit. The lower end of one of the thick wires is connected to the metal cap at the bottom of the bulb whereas the lower end of the other thick wire is connected to the metal case of the bulb.
cards.text_html: The filament of the bulb glows when electricity from a cell is passed through it. The two thick wires inside the glass bulb provide support to the filament and make the connection of the filament to the outer circuit. The lower end of one of the thick wires is connected to the metal cap at the bottom of the bulb whereas the lower end of the other thick wire is connected to the metal case of the bulb.

cards.question: Wire An electric wire is a conducting path in an electric circuit, through which current flows. It is usually made out of a metal that is a good conductor of electricity.
cards.text_html:
Wire
  • An electric wire is a conducting path in an electric circuit, through which current flows.
  • It is usually made out of a metal that is a good conductor of electricity.

cards.question: When the two terminals of a cell are connected to the two terminals of a torch bulb by using wires, the electric current passes through the filament of the bulb gets heated, and starts glowing.
cards.text_html: When the two terminals of a cell are connected to the two terminals of a torch bulb by using wires, the electric current passes through the filament of the bulb gets heated, and starts glowing.

cards.question: Filament: The thin wire that gives off light is called the filament of the bulb.
cards.text_html: Filament: The thin wire that gives off light is called the filament of the bulb.

cards.question: Electric circuit: The path along which electricity can flow is called an electric circuit.
cards.text_html: Electric circuit: The path along which electricity can flow is called an electric circuit.

cards.question: Electric circuit: A closed-loop path, which the current takes is known as an electric circuit. When the path of the circuit is closed, current flows through it. When there is a break in the path (switch is open) then, the circuit is open and not conducting so the current does not flow.
cards.text_html:
Electric circuit:
  • A closed-loop path, which the current takes is known as an electric circuit.
  • When the path of the circuit is closed, current flows through it.
  • When there is a break in the path (switch is open) then, the circuit is open and not conducting so the current does not flow.

cards.question: A closed circuit is an electric circuit in which there is no gap in the connection between the terminals of the cell, wires and bulb, etc. It is also called a complete circuit.
cards.text_html: A closed circuit is an electric circuit in which there is no gap in the connection between the terminals of the cell, wires and bulb, etc. It is also called a complete circuit.

cards.question: An open circuit or an incomplete circuit is an electric circuit in which there is a gap in the connections between the terminal of the cell, wires and bulb, etc. A bulb does not glow when the circuit is open because electricity cannot pass through it.
cards.text_html: An open circuit or an incomplete circuit is an electric circuit in which there is a gap in the connections between the terminal of the cell, wires and bulb, etc. A bulb does not glow when the circuit is open because electricity cannot pass through it.

cards.question: A bulb may fuse due to the following reasons: A bulb may fuse due to the normal wear and tear of the filament. When too much electricity is passed through it suddenly.
cards.text_html:
A bulb may fuse due to the following reasons:
  1. A bulb may fuse due to the normal wear and tear of the filament.
  2. When too much electricity is passed through it suddenly.

cards.question: Flow of current in a circuit: As soon as the path from one terminal of the electric cell to the other is completed, an electric current starts flowing through the circuit and the bulb lights up. The electric current flows from the positive terminal of the electric cell to its negative terminal. ​​​​​​​
cards.text_html:
Flow of current in a circuit: As soon as the path from one terminal of the electric cell to the other is completed, an electric current starts flowing through the circuit and the bulb lights up. The electric current flows from the positive terminal of the electric cell to its negative terminal.

 ​​​​​​​

cards.question: Electric switches: Devices that are used to connect the circuit or break it.
cards.text_html: Electric switches: Devices that are used to connect the circuit or break it.

cards.question: When the switch is connected, we call it ON, and when it’s not in contact (or open) with the circuit, the switch is OFF.​​​​​​
cards.text_html: When the switch is connected, we call it ON, and when it’s not in contact (or open) with the circuit, the switch is OFF.​​​​​​

cards.question: An electric switch is a simple device used to open or close a circuit. An open circuit means that there is a break in the circuit whereas a closed circuit means that there is no gap in the circuit.
cards.text_html: An electric switch is a simple device used to open or close a circuit. An open circuit means that there is a break in the circuit whereas a closed circuit means that there is no gap in the circuit.

cards.question: If in a circuit, the safety pin does not touch the other drawing pin, the switch is in off position due to the gap between them and the electricity does not flow in the circuit.
cards.text_html: If in a circuit, the safety pin does not touch the other drawing pin, the switch is in off position due to the gap between them and the electricity does not flow in the circuit.

cards.question: A torch is a portable electric lamp which uses two or more cells to light a small bulb. A torch is used to provide light in the dark when it is switched on.
cards.text_html: A torch is a portable electric lamp which uses two or more cells to light a small bulb. A torch is used to provide light in the dark when it is switched on.

cards.question: A torch consists of a simple electric circuit in which two or more cells are connected to a torch bulb through a sliding switch. When the torch is needed to provide light, the sliding switch is closed by pushing it forward so that the circuit is completed and the bulb of the torch lights up.
cards.text_html: A torch consists of a simple electric circuit in which two or more cells are connected to a torch bulb through a sliding switch. When the torch is needed to provide light, the sliding switch is closed by pushing it forward so that the circuit is completed and the bulb of the torch lights up.

cards.question: The materials which allow an electric current to pass through them are called conductors. Examples of conductors are coins, keys, pins, aluminium foil, sewing needles, iron nails, binder clips, scissors, etc.
cards.text_html: The materials which allow an electric current to pass through them are called conductors. Examples of conductors are coins, keys, pins, aluminium foil, sewing needles, iron nails, binder clips, scissors, etc.

cards.question: All metals are conductors of electricity. Silver metal is the best conductor of electricity.
cards.text_html:
• All metals are conductors of electricity.
  • Silver metal is the best conductor of electricity.

cards.question: The materials which do not allow an electric current to pass through them are called insulators. Insulators are non-conductors of electricity. Examples of insulators are rubber, plastics, ceramics, wood, glass, mica, cotton, etc.
cards.text_html: The materials which do not allow an electric current to pass through them are called insulators. Insulators are non-conductors of electricity. Examples of insulators are rubber, plastics, ceramics, wood, glass, mica, cotton, etc.

cards.question: The outer covering of electric wires is made up of plastic because plastic is an insulator which does not allow an electric current to pass through it.
cards.text_html: The outer covering of electric wires is made up of plastic because plastic is an insulator which does not allow an electric current to pass through it.

cards.question: Conduction tester: It is a simple device to test whether a material is a conductor or insulator.
cards.text_html: Conduction tester: It is a simple device to test whether a material is a conductor or insulator.
